subject: English
skill: Ask and Answer Questions With Text Evidence

lesson.story_mission: Enter Evidence Explorer Camp, where every answer needs a trail back to the words in the text.
lesson.objective: I can answer literal and inferential questions using evidence from the text.
lesson.concept_explanation: Strong readers notice what the question asks, answer from the text, find exact evidence, and explain how the evidence supports the answer. Literal questions are stated directly. Inferential questions require clues plus thinking.
lesson.common_misconception: A reader may answer from memory, make an inference without evidence, choose an unrelated sentence, or miss the question word.
lesson.real_world_connection: Text evidence helps you discuss books, write reading responses, answer test questions, and support ideas in every subject.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read the question, answer with text support, select evidence, and explain the connection.
lesson.mini_lesson: First identify the question word. Then find the answer or clues in the passage. Finally explain how the evidence proves your answer.

misconception_bank.unsupported_answer: The reader chooses an answer that is not supported by the text.
misconception_bank.inference_without_evidence: The reader makes a reasonable guess but does not connect it to evidence.
misconception_bank.misses_text_evidence: The reader answers correctly but selects weak or unrelated evidence.
misconception_bank.question_word_confusion: The reader mixes up who, what, where, when, why, or how.

story.text: Listen to this page. In this mission, you enter a Grade 4 reading studio for answer literal and inferential questions using evidence from the text. You will learn why the skill matters, watch a model, practice with feedback, and carry your strategy into independent reading. Press Start Mission when you are ready to begin.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This mini lesson teaches answer literal and inferential questions using evidence from the text. First, name the reading job. Next, use the model to notice important words and clues. Finally, answer in a way that matches the text instead of guessing. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This Watch page models the strategy. Listen for the teacher thinking aloud, because strong readers explain what they notice before they answer. Watch how the evidence clue is connected to the correct answer. Press Next to see the demo.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo gives you another guided example. Read the sentence or passage, point to the clue, and decide which answer is best supported. The goal is not speed alone; the goal is careful evidence thinking. Press Next when you are ready to practice.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Practice time. Read the item, use the visual model, and press Read Question if you want the exact prompt read aloud. Press Listen when hearing the word, sentence, or passage will help. Choose or write an answer that fits the text.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Challenge time. These questions ask you to apply answer literal and inferential questions using evidence from the text with less help. Press Read Question for narration, use Listen for pronunciation or passage support, and slow down if two answers seem close. Your best answer should match the evidence.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Checkpoint time. Show that you can use answer literal and inferential questions using evidence from the text independently. Press Read Question if you want the prompt read aloud, and press Listen when audio will help you hear the sentence or passage. Answer carefully, then review your feedback.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your progress. You earned credit for reading closely, using the model, and explaining your evidence thinking. Keep using this strategy whenever you meet a challenging text. Press Next to see your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows your growth and next steps. Review your accuracy, hints, and level progress. If one level needs more work, choose Practice This Skill and return to that level in the Skill Practice Center.
profile.label: Read This Page
